question_title: Will Rajasthan's State Election Commission announce the panchayat (PRI) general election schedule before July 25, 2026?
summary: Rajasthan's OBC door-to-door survey runs July 10-23, 2026, with the reservation report compiled and submitted only AFTER July 23. The SEC has stated it needs ~90 days after reservation is finalized before conducting polls, and it explicitly awaits reservation notifications before announcing any schedule. With the prerequisite process not concluding until the very end of the resolution window, announcing an actual PRI polling date before 23:59 IST July 24 is highly improbable. Reports indicate polls may slip to September-October. Small residual probability remains for a surprise announcement under High Court pressure (July 31 deadline) using the fallback of treating OBC seats as general.

factors.description: OBC survey runs July 10-23; report compiled/submitted only after July 23; SEC schedule awaits reservation finalization
SEC has stated it needs at least 90 days after reservation process complete to conduct elections
Status quo: no schedule announced, repeated delays, reports of slip to Sept-Oct
HC pressure (July 31 deadline) plus fallback option creates small chance of a sudden announcement

reasoning:
**Forecast: 5% (NO)**

The decisive fact is timing: Rajasthan's OBC reservation survey runs July 10–23, 2026, with the report compiled and submitted only *after* July 23. Since the SEC explicitly awaits reservation notifications before announcing a schedule—and has said it needs ~90 days lead time—there is virtually no path to announcing an actual PRI polling date within the July 11–24 window. The residual 5% accounts for a surprise announcement under High Court pressure using the fallback of treating OBC-reserved seats as general.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if, between July 11, 2026 and 23:59 IST on July 24, 2026, the State Election Commission of Rajasthan formally announces (issues a press note or notification containing) the polling date(s) for the general election to Panchayati Raj Institutions (gram panchayats / zila parishads / panchayat samitis) in Rajasthan. The announcement must specify at least one actual date of poll for the PRI general election. Resolution is based on official notifications on the SEC Rajasthan website (https://sec.rajasthan.gov.in/) or reporting by [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions). If no such schedule with a polling date is announced within the window, the question resolves NO.
fine_print: The announcement must fix at least one specific date of poll for the Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRI) general election. A mere statement of intent, a reservation notification, a ward-demarcation notice, or an announcement of only nomination/preparatory dates without any polling date does not count. If the election schedule announced covers only some tiers or phases of the PRI general election but includes at least one polling date, that counts as YES. Announcements relating solely to urban local body (ULB / municipal) elections do not count.

factors.description: OBC survey runs July 10-23, report compiled and submitted only after July 23; SEC schedule awaits reservation finalization
SEC has stated it needs at least 90 days after reservation process complete to conduct elections
Status quo: no schedule announced, repeated delays, reports of slip to Sept-Oct
HC pressure to complete by July 31 plus fallback option creates small chance of sudden announcement

input.anchor: Base rate for a delayed, contested election schedule being announced within a specific 11-day window while prerequisites incomplete: low, ~10%
input.assessment: Short 11-day window. The reservation process (OBC survey) doesn't even conclude until July 23, and the report is submitted afterward. SEC explicitly awaits reservation notifications before announcing a schedule and needs ~90 days lead time. Announcing an actual polling date before July 24 is very unlikely. Small residual probability for a surprise announcement under HC pressure or use of the fallback (treating OBC seats as general).
input.tool_audit: web_search returned key facts: OBC survey July 10-23 timeline (newkerala), SC/HC April 2026 completion assurances, panchayattimes on delay causes. Sufficient for a confident NO.
input.process_reflection: Straightforward short-window status-quo question. Web search quickly surfaced the decisive timeline fact (survey ends July 23). No need for heavy simulation.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
The forecast's core evidence chain is well-grounded and the 5% probability is sound. I independently estimate ~4–8%, consistent with the agent.

VERIFIED IN TRACE:
- Factor 1 (OBC survey July 10–23, report submitted only after July 23): directly supported by the newkerala snippet ("survey is scheduled to continue until July 23, after which the Commission will compile its findings and submit a report to the state government").
- Factor 2 (SEC needs ~90 days after reservation finalized): supported by the panchayattimes Hindi snippet stating the commission needs at least 90 days, counted only after SC/ST/OBC/women reservation is complete.
- Factor 3 core claim (repeated delays, no schedule yet): supported by panchayattimes on delay causes and the "31 जुलाई तक नहीं होंगे" framing.
- Factor 4 / counterargument (HC July 31 deadline, deferral context): supported by the panchayattimes snippet "राजस्थान हाईकोर्ट ने 22 मई को अपने आदेश में 31 जुलाई तक पंचा...".

Signs are all correct (three barriers negative; HC-pressure factor positive). Resolution alignment is good: factors engage the specific requirement of a fixed polling date within the July 11–24 window and correctly exclude preparatory/reservation steps per the fine print. Resolution criteria were provided (not missing). No pre-publication-event problem (this is a forward-looking "will X happen before Y" question and the agent treats it as such). No regime-window issue (not numeric). Counterargument is genuinely strong — it builds a coherent YES pathway (HC hard deadline + fallback + short-notice SEC behavior).

WARN-LEVEL ISSUES:
1. Tool-audit narrative discrepancy. The agent's tool_audit says the run was clean and "Sufficient for a confident NO," but ground-truth metrics show 2 research calls at 100% error rate and a 20% session error rate. The narrative doesn't disclose these failures. Minor, since the web_searches carried the evidence, but the audit overstates a clean run.
2. Two non-load-bearing details are not visible in the rendered trace: the "fallback option (treating OBC-reserved seats as general seats)" in factor 4/counterargument, and "reports of slip to Sept-Oct" in factor 3. Note the trace renders only 2 of 6 web_search calls (4 are uncaptured), so these likely come from uncaptured searches. Neither is load-bearing — factor 4 is only +0.6 and factor 3's core (ongoing delays) is independently supported — so this doesn't affect direction.
3. Slightly overconfident self-assessment: confidence_in_estimate = 0.85 is a touch high given the live HC July-31 deadline, the unverified fallback pathway the agent itself cites, and the fact that Indian SECs can notify on short notice. 0.75–0.80 would be better calibrated.

None of these change the forecast direction; the 5% estimate is well-supported.
